extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN Tom won Giro d'Italia by 31 seconds over NIBALI Vincenzo after dominating Stage 21 ITT, overturning 53-second deficit to QUINTANA Nairo.
tactical_patterns: DUMOULIN overcame 53s deficit to QUINTANA in final ITT, revealing Colombian's chronic time trial vulnerability in Grand Tour calculus.
VAN EMDEN Jos placed second on stage, demonstrating Sunweb depth in ITT discipline for future three-week race support.
QUINTANA lost 53+ seconds in sub-30km ITT despite holding pink, confirming need for 90+ second cushions before flat TTs.
PINOT Thibaut faded badly in final ITT, suggesting mental fragility under pressure when podium positions still contested.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Crushed final ITT by half-minute margins over specialists, proving Grand Tour ITT dominance under maximum pressure.
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: Held second overall but lost 25+ seconds to DUMOULIN in ITT, confirming his time trialing ceiling against specialists.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Collapsed from pink jersey to third, losing 53+ seconds in short ITT exposes fatal flaw in stage race armor.
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: Poor ITT performance under podium pressure suggests psychological weakness in high-stakes final time trials.
rider_signals.VAN EMDEN Jos: positive: Stage victory demonstrates he's genuine ITT threat capable of Grand Tour stage wins when released from domestique duties.
